articles.title: The creator of Roomba is back with a furry robot companion
articles.summary: Colin Angle, creator of Roomba, launched a new startup with a furry robot companion designed to interact autonomously with families at home, shifting from cleaning robots to companion robots.

articles.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
articles.summary: Elon Musk and Sam Altman are in a high-stakes trial over OpenAI's direction. Musk alleges OpenAI abandoned its mission to benefit humanity in favor of profit.

articles.title: How the internet’s favorite squirrel dad made the hottest camera app of 2026
articles.summary: DualShot Recorder became the App Store's top paid app in 12 hours. It was created by Derrick Downey Jr., an internet personality famous for filming squirrels on his LA patio.

articles.title: Elon Musk’s only expert witness at the OpenAI trial fears an AGI arms race
articles.summary: Stuart Russell, an AI researcher, testified as Elon Musk's expert witness at the OpenAI trial, expressing concerns about AGI arms races and advocating for government regulation of frontier AI labs.

articles.title: Sierra raises $950M as the race to own enterprise AI gets serious
articles.summary: Sierra raised $950M, bringing its total to over $1B. The company plans to use the capital to become the global standard for AI-powered customer experiences.

articles.title: Elon Musk sent ominous texts to Greg Brockman, Sam Altman after asking for a settlement, OpenAI claims
articles.summary: Elon Musk allegedly sent threatening texts to OpenAI leaders Greg Brockman and Sam Altman after requesting a settlement, warning they would become 'the most hated men in America.'

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud. The funding targets developer frustration with legacy cloud complexity and cost.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing has sparked developer backlash. Goose, a free open-source alternative by Block, offers similar autonomous coding capabilities without subscription fees or cloud dependency.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge. The unconventional recruitment stunt successfully attracted thousands of engineers to compete for positions.

articles.title: Ubuntu infrastructure has been down for more than a day
articles.summary: Ubuntu and Canonical servers have been offline for over 24 hours due to a sustained cross-border cyberattack, preventing OS updates and normal communications following a major vulnerability disclosure.

articles.title: The most severe Linux threat to surface in years catches the world flat-footed
articles.summary: A critical Linux vulnerability allowing root access was publicly exploited after researchers released code Wednesday, catching most distributions unprepared despite patches available in several kernel versions.

articles.title: Why a recent supply-chain attack singled out security firms Checkmarx and Bitwarden
articles.summary: Security firm Checkmarx suffered multiple supply-chain attacks within six weeks, including a Trivy vulnerability scanner breach and compromise of its own GitHub account that delivered malware to customers twice.

articles.title: Tailoring AI solutions for health care needs
articles.summary: AI developers target healthcare's financial and labor challenges through diverse applications from cancer treatment to administrative automation. Success requires understanding healthcare's complex environment, as many vendors have failed by misunderstanding the sector.

articles.title: Musk v. Altman week 1: Elon Musk says he was duped, warns AI could kill us all, and admits that xAI distills OpenAI’s models
articles.summary: In the Musk v. OpenAI trial, Elon Musk claimed he was deceived by Sam Altman and warned AI poses existential risks. He admitted his xAI company uses OpenAI's models for training.

articles.title: Cyber-Insecurity in the AI Era
articles.summary: AI expands cybersecurity attack surfaces and complexity, making legacy security approaches inadequate. Security must be fundamentally rethought with AI at its core rather than added afterward.

articles.title: AI evals are becoming the new compute bottleneck
articles.summary: AI evaluation has emerged as a major bottleneck in AI development, rivaling compute resources as a limiting factor. Organizations struggle with assessing model performance at scale.

articles.title: Granite 4.1 LLMs: How They’re Built
articles.summary: Granite 4.1 LLMs represent advances in large language model architecture and training methods. The article explores the technical foundation and construction techniques behind these models.

articles.title: DeepInfra on Hugging Face Inference Providers 🔥
articles.summary: DeepInfra is now available as an inference provider on Hugging Face, enabling users to access powerful AI models through their platform integration.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI development and emerging trends in artificial intelligence technology.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to boost productivity and creativity in content creation and handling tasks.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft open sources its 'farm of the future' toolkit, enabling broader access to agricultural technology innovations.

articles.title: The latest AI news we announced in April 2026
articles.summary: Google announced its latest AI updates in April 2026, showcasing new developments and features across its artificial intelligence products and services.

articles.title: Celebrating 20 years of Google Translate: Fun facts, tips and new features to try
articles.summary: Google Translate celebrates 20 years, evolving from a 2006 AI experiment to supporting nearly 250 languages. The company shares fun facts and new features marking the milestone.

articles.title: Join the new AI Agents Vibe Coding Course from Google and Kaggle
articles.summary: Google and Kaggle are offering a new 5-Day AI Agents Intensive Course called Vibe Coding. Registration is now open for interested participants.

articles.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
articles.summary: Researchers are developing an AI co-clinician to augment healthcare delivery through AI-assisted care models. This advancement aims to enhance clinical decision-making and patient outcomes.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with the Republic of Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using advanced AI models.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo introduces a new method for distributed AI training that improves resilience and efficiency. The approach enables more robust model training across multiple systems with better fault tolerance.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #243 - GPT 5.5, DeepSeek V4, AI safety sabotage
articles.summary: The podcast discusses OpenAI's GPT-5.5 release with coding improvements and higher pricing, xAI's Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 for real-time voice agents, and concerns about AI safety sabotage.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
articles.summary: OpenAI released ChatGPT Images 2.0 with improved text and screenshot generation. The podcast discusses this update alongside Qwen 3.6 Max and Kimi-K2.6 developments.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced Small 4 model family with mixture-of-experts architecture.

articles.title: Physical AI raises governance questions for autonomous systems
articles.summary: Physical AI governance faces challenges as autonomous systems integrate into robots and industrial equipment. Testing, monitoring, and control of real-world AI interactions require new frameworks amid rapidly growing robot installations.

articles.title: Google made agentic AI governance a product. Enterprises still have to catch up.
articles.summary: Google launched the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform at Cloud Next '26, making agentic AI governance a native product feature with built-in cryptographic identity and oversight capabilities for enterprise adoption.

articles.title: SAP: How enterprise AI governance secures profit margins
articles.summary: Enterprise AI governance replaces statistical guesses with deterministic control to secure profit margins. Organizations must prioritize precision, governance, and scalability as AI models move into production environments.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, advancing understanding of collaborative AI system debugging.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture that improves autonomous robot navigation by addressing localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals that AI coding assistants like Claude Code are inadvertently leaking sensitive API keys to public code repositories and package registries.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeover attacks, representing a major supply chain vulnerability.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distilling LLMs for faster, concise responses reduces their ability to explore alternative solutions on difficult problems, revealing a tradeoff between speed and generalization.

articles.title: 5 MacOS command line tools I swear by over their GUI counterparts
articles.summary: The article recommends five macOS command-line tools that offer greater efficiency than their graphical counterparts, encouraging users to explore terminal applications for improved productivity.

articles.title: Hundreds of readers bought these headphones this year (and they're not from Bose, Sony, or Apple)
articles.summary: Readers surprisingly preferred headphones from brands other than Bose, Sony, or Apple. A tech reviewer's year-long testing revealed unexpected consumer favorites.

articles.title: How this travel company's AI rollout drove a 73% satisfaction boost: A 5-step playbook for your business
articles.summary: A travel company achieved 73% satisfaction improvement through structured AI implementation. The article provides a 5-step playbook to help businesses successfully deploy AI initiatives beyond initial exploration.

articles.title: Agentic RAG Explained in 3 Levels of Difficulty
articles.summary: Agentic RAG combines retrieval-augmented generation with autonomous agents for intelligent decision-making. The article explains this concept across three difficulty levels for different audiences.

articles.title: Effective KV Compression with TurboQuant
articles.summary: Google launched TurboQuant, a new algorithmic suite for quantizing and compressing large language models and vector search engines used in RAG systems.

articles.title: Building AI Agents in Python with Pydantic AI
articles.summary: Pydantic AI enables developers to build AI agents in Python using structured data validation. The framework simplifies agent development with built-in tools for LLM integration and reliable outputs.

articles.title: Playing Connect Four with Deep Q-Learning
articles.summary: Researchers demonstrate solving the multiplayer game Connect Four using Deep Q-Learning with function approximation techniques.

articles.title: How AI Tools Generate Technical Debt in IoT Systems — and What to Do About It
articles.summary: AI tools accelerate IoT development but risk creating technical debt when buggy code simultaneously breaks thousands of devices. Proper testing and validation strategies are essential for hardware-level reliability.

articles.title: CSPNet Paper Walkthrough: Just Better, No Tradeoffs
articles.summary: CSPNet improves neural network efficiency through Cross-Stage Partial connections without performance tradeoffs. The article reviews the paper and provides a PyTorch implementation.

articles.title: Nemotron Labs: What OpenClaw Agents Mean for Every Organization
articles.summary: OpenClaw, an open-source AI agents project, reached 100,000 GitHub stars by early 2026, becoming a phenomenon. The NVIDIA Nemotron Labs series explores how open models and datasets help organizations build specialized AI systems efficiently.

articles.title: It’s Gonna Be May: 16 Games Hit the Cloud This Month, With More NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080 Power
articles.summary: 16 games are joining NVIDIA GeForce NOW in May, including AAA titles like Forza Horizon 6 and 007 First Light. RTX 5080 power is now available for cloud gaming across devices.

articles.title: NVIDIA Launches Nemotron 3 Nano Omni Model, Unifying Vision, Audio and Language for up to 9x More Efficient AI Agents
articles.summary: NVIDIA launched Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, a unified multimodal AI model combining vision, audio, and language capabilities into one system, delivering 9x efficiency gains for AI agents.

articles.title: Introducing the agent performance loop: AgentCore Optimization now in preview
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore now offers automated performance optimization for AI agents, enabling teams to generate recommendations from production data, validate with testing, and deploy updates confidently while preventing quality degradation.

articles.title: Agent-guided workflows to accelerate model customization in Amazon SageMaker AI
articles.summary: Amazon SageMaker AI introduces agent-guided workflows that simplify model customization by automating fine-tuning, data preparation, and evaluation through natural language descriptions, reducing complexity for developers.

articles.title: Generate dashboards from natural language prompts in Amazon Quick
articles.summary: Amazon QuickSight now generates complete dashboards from natural language prompts, enabling users to create production-ready analyses with visualizations and filters in minutes instead of hours.

articles.title: Speed Up Unreal Engine NNE Inference with NVIDIA TensorRT for RTX Runtime
articles.summary: NVIDIA TensorRT integration with Unreal Engine NNE accelerates neural network inference on RTX hardware, enabling faster super resolution, denoising, and neural rendering for improved graphics performance.

articles.title: Build AI-Powered Games with NVIDIA DLSS 4.5, RTX, and Unreal Engine 5
articles.summary: NVIDIA releases DLSS 4.5 with Dynamic Multi Frame Generation and enhanced Super Resolution for game developers using Unreal Engine 5 to create advanced AI-powered games.

articles.title: How to Build, Run, and Scale High-Quality Creator Workflows in ComfyUI
articles.summary: ComfyUI is an open-source, node-based tool that uses generative AI to accelerate creative workflows, automating repetitive tasks and enabling lean teams to produce more assets efficiently.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: Research study examines how generational differences shape consumer attitudes toward social media ads across platforms, conducted in collaboration with CrowdDNA.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and sharing these solutions openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with unconventional background pioneered innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experience drives breakthrough development in immersive computing.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for learned world models enabling long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic exploration, and improved gradient shaping for high-dimensional vision inputs.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers propose methods for identifying interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and transparency. This work aims to make LLM decision-making processes more understandable for safer, more trustworthy AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses an encoder to map objects to images, with noise corruption in measurements. An information estimator quantifies measurement quality using only noisy data and noise models.
